opportunities.name: National Lottery Awards for All England
opportunities.description: Grassroots and community activity funding to build strong relationships in communities, improve spaces that matter, and help people reach their potential. Supports groups facing cost-of-living challenges.
opportunities.funder_priorities: The National Lottery Community Fund focuses on supporting people facing poverty, disadvantage or discrimination. Their strategy aims to create stronger, fairer communities with four missions: communities come together, children and young people thrive, communities are healthier, and tackling climate change.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Activities that benefit and involve the community including running costs, salaries, equipment, volunteers, training, rent and bills. Projects up to 2 years. Prioritises groups with smaller incomes.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Projects outside England, activities primarily the responsibility of statutory agencies, projects that should be funded by government, retrospective funding, or where reserves are outside policy.
opportunities.application_process: Rolling applications online. Decision within 16 weeks. Can apply anytime. Simple online application form. Do not need to be registered charity but need bank account requiring two signatories.

opportunities.name: Leathersellers' Charity - Adverse Childhood Experiences Programme
opportunities.description: Grants to UK-registered charities that support children and young people recovering from Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACEs) and parents affected by ACEs, mental illness or addiction.
opportunities.funder_priorities: The Leathersellers' Company aims to prevent and address the harm of adverse childhood experiences. Now in its fifth year, focuses on helping children overcome early trauma and supporting parents affected by mental health issues or addiction.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Direct service delivery supporting children and young people recovering from ACEs, parenting support programmes, mental health interventions, and projects addressing addiction's impact on families.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Non-registered charities, organisations outside the UK, capital projects only, research without direct service delivery, or retrospective funding.
opportunities.application_process: Rolling programme. Complete eligibility check online. If eligible, full application required. Main programme has specific deadlines throughout the year.

opportunities.name: Leathersellers' Small Grants Programme
opportunities.description: Rolling funding programme for one-off grants to small charities with annual income under £200k and holding less than six months' reserves. Supports ACEs-focused work.
opportunities.deadline: Rolling - 8 windows per year, closes when 40 applications received
opportunities.funder_priorities: Focused specifically on small charities working with children affected by adverse childhood experiences. Values organisations working at grassroots level with limited financial reserves who need quick access to funding.
opportunities.what_they_fund: One-off grants for small projects, equipment, training for staff working with vulnerable children, pilot programmes, or specific interventions addressing childhood trauma.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Large charities over £200k income, those with reserves exceeding six months' running costs, capital building projects, or general running costs.
opportunities.application_process: Eight application windows throughout year. Each window closes when 40 applications received. Can close within 20 minutes. Apply at 10am on opening day. Quick turnaround decision.

opportunities.name: Asda Foundation - Local Community Spaces Fund
opportunities.description: Funding for safe and welcoming community spaces that bring people together, connect communities and address loneliness and isolation. Must be accessible community venues.
opportunities.funder_priorities: The Asda Foundation prioritizes creating safe, welcoming spaces that bring people together. Focus on tackling loneliness and isolation by supporting community hubs where diverse groups can connect and participate in activities.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Community centres, halls, and spaces that provide meeting places for local people. Running costs to maintain these spaces, activities that use the space to address isolation, and improvements to make spaces more welcoming.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Individual projects without a fixed community space, activities that don't directly address community connection and loneliness, or spaces not accessible to the wider community.
opportunities.application_process: Single-stage application online. Deadline 28 April 2026. Submit detailed application form with supporting documents. Decisions announced after assessment period.

opportunities.name: AB Charitable Trust - Open Programme
opportunities.description: Unrestricted grants for registered charities promoting human dignity and defending human rights of marginalised people. Focus on Human Rights Framework, Access to Justice, Criminal Legal System Reform, and Migrants/Refugees.
opportunities.amount: £10,000-£30,000 per year for up to 3 years
opportunities.funder_priorities: AB Charitable Trust champions human dignity and human rights of marginalized and excluded people. Particularly interested in access to justice, criminal legal system reform, and supporting migrants and refugees. Values systemic change approaches.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Unrestricted grants for core costs including salaries, rent, overheads. Supports advocacy, legal support, systemic reform work, direct services for marginalized groups, and organizations led by people with lived experience.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Charities with income outside £150,000-£1.5 million range, capital projects only, or work outside their four priority areas.
opportunities.application_process: Online application with deadline Friday 24 April 2026. Two-stage process possible. Unrestricted funding means can be used for any organizational purpose within charitable objectives.

opportunities.name: The Fore - Unrestricted Funding
opportunities.description: Unrestricted grants to help small charities and CICs grow, strengthen, become more efficient or resilient. Includes free skilled professional support alongside funding.
opportunities.funder_priorities: The Fore focuses on strengthening small charities and social enterprises to help them grow sustainably. They look for 'excellent' small organizations ready to take the next step. Value innovation, scalability, and organizational development over ongoing operations.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Unrestricted funding for salaries, new staff posts, systems, technology, training, strategic planning, diversifying income. Must strengthen organization internally and help it grow or become more sustainable. Not for ongoing operations.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Simply maintaining current services, capital projects only, large organizations, or organizations not demonstrating readiness to grow.
opportunities.application_process: Register interest during one-week window (8-15 July 2026). If invited, submit full application. Includes professional support matching alongside grant. Assessment includes interview stage.

opportunities.name: King Charles III Charitable Fund - Small Grants Programme
opportunities.description: Grants to UK nonprofits for projects in environment, countryside, education, heritage & conservation, social inclusion and health & wellbeing. Focus on disadvantaged communities.
opportunities.amount: Up to £3,000 per year for up to 3 years
opportunities.funder_priorities: Guided by the Founder's interests, placing special focus on initiatives working with most disadvantaged communities—both urban and rural. Aims to create opportunities and drive positive change. Values raising awareness of critical social and environmental issues.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Project costs for work in six themes: environment, countryside, education, heritage and conservation, social inclusion, health and wellbeing. Priority to disadvantaged communities using Index of Multiple Deprivation.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Capital expenditure except community heritage/conservation for historic buildings. Food waste/insecurity (separate Coronation Food Project). Organizations with late reporting. Work duplicating other royal charities.
opportunities.application_process: Complete eligibility quiz to receive unique application link. Online application when fund is open. Two-stage process. Annual income between £25,000-£500,000 required.

opportunities.name: Greggs Foundation - Community Action Fund Round 3
opportunities.description: Three-year core funding grants for organizations in geographical focus areas helping communities tackle local challenges. Supports access to community services, reducing isolation, building confidence and opportunities.
opportunities.amount: £20,000 per year for up to 3 years
opportunities.funder_priorities: Greggs Foundation prioritizes organizations supporting people to access effective community services to widen networks, reduce isolation and build knowledge, confidence and opportunities. Focus on areas near Greggs outlets or areas of need. Core funding approach recognizing importance of organizational sustainability.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Core running costs including salaries, rent, utilities, and fixed costs to keep services running. Organizations must demonstrate clear understanding of local issues and deliver programmes making positive impact. Education, health, ex-offenders, homelessness, poverty, arts, environment, older people, disability inclusion.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Organizations outside geographical focus areas for each round. Income outside £25,000-£1 million range. More than 6 months free reserves. Fewer than 3 unrelated trustees. Not delivering significant proportion of services in focus area.
opportunities.application_process: Round 3 open for specific geographical locations only. Online application closing 8 May 2026 noon. Highly competitive—approximately 13% success rate. Must submit latest accounts, current year budget, cost breakdown.

opportunities.name: Grocers' Charity Grant
opportunities.description: One-off grants for UK-registered charities in nine sectors: relief of hardship, children & young people, elderly, disability, health, military, arts, heritage, and environment. Focus on areas of high deprivation.
opportunities.amount: Up to £5,000 (larger amounts for exceptional impact)
opportunities.funder_priorities: The Grocers' Charity supports tackling social issues across nine sectors with £1 million yearly. Focus on charities serving UK communities, especially in areas of high deprivation. Values practical, ground-level projects making real difference to disadvantaged people.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Direct project costs for training programmes, better facilities, services ending loneliness in elderly, disability inclusion, wellbeing support for children, employment help for veterans, environmental sustainability, habitat restoration. Equipment, running events, training staff.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Places of worship, schools, colleges, universities, individuals, non-UK organizations including CICs, organizations with overseas beneficiaries, charities over income limits (£500,000 or £15m for health).
opportunities.application_process: Two-stage system. Stage 1: Online eligibility checklist and enquiry form. Reply within 3 weeks. Stage 2: Only invited groups submit detailed form within one week. Trustees decide. Deadline 1 September 2026.

opportunities.name: UnLtd - Millennium Awards Trust Programme
opportunities.description: Funding for UK-based social entrepreneurs aged 16+ to start or grow their social venture. Two tiers: Start awards up to £8,000 and Grow awards up to £18,000. Community-focused business ventures.
opportunities.funder_priorities: UnLtd supports social entrepreneurs creating positive social change through business approaches. Values innovation, community impact, and supporting people to develop sustainable social ventures. Focus on entrepreneurs from diverse backgrounds and those addressing disadvantage.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Start-up costs for new social ventures, growth funding for existing community enterprises, business development, marketing, equipment, pilot projects. Focus on ventures creating social impact through sustainable business models.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Traditional charities not operating as social enterprises, projects without clear social mission, ventures not led by applicant, retrospective costs.
opportunities.application_process: Rolling applications online. Two funding tiers available. Complete application form detailing social venture, impact plans, and budget. Assessment process includes interview. Support and mentoring included with awards.

opportunities.name: Better Community Business Network Grant Initiative
opportunities.description: Small grants supporting charities and community-led projects across UK improving quality of life for individuals and communities, particularly those experiencing social or economic disadvantage.
opportunities.funder_priorities: BCBN focuses on improving quality of life and opportunities for individuals and communities experiencing social or economic disadvantage. Values community-led approaches and grassroots organizations working directly with beneficiaries.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Education projects including schools and extra-curricular clubs, health initiatives covering mental wellbeing and healthy eating, services for ex-offenders including mentoring and rehabilitation, homelessness and poverty work, arts and cultural activities, environmental projects, services for older people, disability inclusion and social cohesion.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Organizations not registered with Charity Commission or Companies House for at least 18 months, those without submitted annual accounts, or projects outside the UK.
opportunities.application_process: Must be based in UK and registered with recognized governing body for 18+ months. Submit by deadlines: 17 April 2026, 7 September 2026, or 9 November 2026. One-off awards, total £36,000 annual allocation through 12 grants yearly.

opportunities.name: D'Oyly Carte Charitable Trust
opportunities.description: Grants for UK charities supporting performing arts, creative health and heritage crafts. Unrestricted support available for charities under £250,000 income. Prioritizes improving access for communities facing barriers.
opportunities.funder_priorities: D'Oyly Carte focuses on performing arts, creative health using arts for wellbeing, and traditional heritage crafts. Prioritizes work improving access for communities facing identifiable barriers to opportunity. Values arts as tool for social inclusion and health improvement.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Performing arts projects, creative health programmes using arts for wellbeing, heritage crafts training and preservation, community arts access programmes. Unrestricted support for small charities under £250k income. Projects demonstrating inclusive practice.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Non-UK registered charities, work not benefiting UK residents, projects without clear alignment to funding themes, capital building projects only.
opportunities.application_process: Rolling applications. UK registered or regulated exempt charities only. Assessment criteria include alignment with themes, beneficiary identification, delivery capability, financial management, inclusive practice and safeguarding arrangements.

opportunities.name: £10 Million UK Youth Fund
opportunities.description: Multi-year unrestricted grants for small youth and outdoor learning organizations across UK. Aims to improve financial resilience and sustainability of youth sector organizations over several years.
opportunities.amount: Unrestricted grants over several years (amounts vary)
opportunities.funder_priorities: Major new £10 million programme targeting small youth and outdoor learning organizations. Focus on improving financial resilience and long-term sustainability of youth sector. Recognizes need for unrestricted multi-year funding to strengthen organizations working with young people.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Unrestricted funding for core costs, organizational development, sustainability planning, capacity building. Youth work programmes, outdoor learning activities, youth development services. Multi-year funding to provide stability.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Large youth organizations with significant reserves, statutory bodies, projects outside youth and outdoor learning focus.
opportunities.application_process: New funding round opened April 2026. Application process through National Lottery Community Fund. Multi-year approach meaning longer-term partnership. Designed specifically for small organizations.

opportunities.name: Skinners' Charity Foundation - Young People's Vocational Development
opportunities.description: Grants for organizations helping NEET young people get ready for and move into employment. Supports vocational training and development. Focus on London and Kent with some funding in other Kent areas.
opportunities.amount: Up to £10,000 per year (3 years considered)
opportunities.funder_priorities: Skinners' Charity focuses on helping young people not in education, employment or training (NEET) move into sustainable employment. Values vocational training pathways, work readiness programmes, and supporting young people from low-income backgrounds overcome poverty and disadvantage.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Vocational training programmes, employment readiness schemes, CSCS training potentially, skills development for NEET youth aged 16-30, mentoring and support to help young people access work, apprenticeship support, work placements.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Projects outside London and Kent focus areas, work not directly supporting employment outcomes for young people, organizations without track record in youth employment.
opportunities.application_process: Registered charities and not-for-profit organizations in London and Kent can apply. Rolling programme. Awards for three years open to consideration. Also offers Relief of Poverty and Hardship Programme up to £5,000/year.

opportunities.name: CITB Employer Networks Funding
opportunities.description: Construction training funding for employers with 1-249 employees. Covers short course training including CSCS, plant, scaffolding and health & safety. Major changes from January 2026 with new caps by employer size.
opportunities.funder_priorities: CITB focuses on developing skills in construction industry. Employer Networks programme supports SME employers to train workforce. Reformed in 2026 to spread funding more sustainably across sector. Focus on health & safety, plant operations, scaffolding and core construction skills.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Short course construction training including CSCS cards, plant operations, scaffolding tickets, health and safety courses. Funded at 30% of average market rate. For employers with 1-249 employees registered with CITB and up-to-date with levy.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Large employers 250+ (separate Large Employer Fund for 2026-27 only). Long qualifications moved to grants scheme. Level 7 qualifications. Employers not registered with CITB or in arrears with levy.
opportunities.application_process: Register with CITB. Ensure levy payments up to date. Book training with approved training providers. Funding confirmed at booking. Annual budget £11.5 million for 2026-27 with caps by employer size. Rolling throughout year until budget exhausted.
